existing_Entity_Description: Tofino is a municipality in Vancouver Island Central & North, British Columbia, Canada, within the Alberni-Clayoquot. As of the 2021 Canadian Census, Tofino had a population of 2,516. The community is situated in a stunning mix of coastal communities, old-growth forests, and rugged Pacific sh. a growing tourism economy with seasonal peaks in summer. The region features mild coastal climate tempered by the Pacific with abundant rainfall.
existing_Local_Facts:
• Population: 2,516 (2021 Census)
• Region: Vancouver Island Central & North
• Regional District: Alberni-Clayoquot
• Type: Municipality
• Climate: mild coastal climate tempered by the Pacific with abundant rainfall
• Character: outdoor recreation paradise — surfing, fishing, hiking, and wildlife viewing
• Distance from Vancouver: ~203 km

REGION_BUNDLE.climate: Wet coastal temperate rainforest climate, wetter and windier moving north and to the outer coast; Tofino/Ucluelet face open-Pacific storms (storm-watching season); inland east-coast communities like the Comox Valley are drier and sunnier.
REGION_BUNDLE.terrain: The Vancouver Island Ranges spine, old-growth rainforest, long fjords and inlets, and the sheltered east-coast plain along the Strait of Georgia where most communities sit.
REGION_BUNDLE.economy: Forestry heritage transitioning to aquaculture, tourism, retirees and remote work; Nanaimo is the island's transport hub (two ferry terminals to the mainland); Comox hosts CFB Comox; Port Alberni, Campbell River and Port Hardy anchor resource industries.
REGION_BUNDLE.building_stock: Post-war mill-town houses, 1970s-90s ranchers, waterfront and view homes, and fast-growing retirement-driven subdivisions in Nanaimo, Parksville-Qualicum and the Comox Valley.
